name: 30-Day Waiting Period
description: All benefits-eligible employees must complete a 30-day waiting period before coverage becomes effective.
conflictNote: Conflicts with BR-002. Guide says '30 days'. Eligibility policy says 'first of month following 30 days'. These produce different effective dates.

name: First-of-Month Coverage Start
description: Coverage begins on the first day of the month following the completion of the waiting period.
conflictNote: Conflicts with BR-001. See analysis: employee hired Nov 5 — BR-001 gives coverage Dec 6, BR-002 gives coverage Jan 1. Delta = 26 days.

name: HSA-HDHP Coupling Rule
description: Employees may contribute to a Health Savings Account (HSA) only if they are enrolled in a qualifying High Deductible Health Plan (HDHP). Enrollment in any non-HDHP medical plan disqualifies HSA contributions.

name: Life Event 30-Day Enrollment Window
description: When an employee experiences a qualifying life event (marriage, birth, adoption, divorce, loss of other coverage), they have 30 days from the event date to make changes to their benefit elections.

name: Voluntary Life EOI Threshold
description: Voluntary Life coverage above $100,000 requires Evidence of Insurability (EOI). Coverage amounts at or below $100,000 are available as a guaranteed issue during initial enrollment.

name: Dependent Age-26 Cutoff
description: Dependent children lose eligibility at the end of the month in which they turn 26. Automatic termination notice must be sent 90 days prior to the birthday.

name: Notification Timeliness — AMBIGUOUS
description: Employees must notify HR of qualifying life events promptly. 'Promptly' is not defined in the source document.
ambiguityNote: The word 'promptly' is undefined. Best practice is 30 days; IRS rules allow 30 days for some events; HIPAA allows 30 days. AI should flag for clarification and recommend defining 'promptly' as 30 calendar days.
